metadata.title: Why Your Brain Feels No Pain When You Tap a Card
metadata.description: You tap a card every day without a second thought. Here's the real reason that silence at checkout isn't an accident.

voiceover.directive: Calm, curious, a little conspiratorial. Unhurried.
voiceover.full_text: This morning, you tapped a small piece of plastic against a machine. Money left your account. You felt nothing at all. Economists call that missing flicker the pain of paying. Your brain evolved to feel a small sting every time you spend. A tap removes that sting on purpose, not by accident. That silence at checkout is proof the system worked exactly as intended.

timeline.text: This morning, you tapped a small piece of plastic against a machine.
